channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alcohol tax reforms aim to curb excessive consumption and illicit trade
item.description: The South African alcohol industry has urged the National Treasury to link new excise levies to consumer price inflation and incentivize production of lower-strength alcoholic drinks to address abuse ...

item.title: Proposed Magna Carta Aims to Protect Informal Vendors' Rights
item.description: Mamamayang Liberal Party-list Representative Leila de Lima has proposed a Magna Carta for informal ambulant vendors to address their challenges and protect their rights. The bill, House Bill No. 10043...

item.title: Celebrated actor Terence Donovan passes away at 90, remembered for iconic TV roles
item.description: Renowned British-Australian actor Terence Donovan, best known for his iconic role as Doug Willis in the long-running soap Neighbours, has passed away at the age of 90. His death was announced by his s...

item.title: Sprinkler Issues Threaten Greenery at GCC Park in Kalakshetra Colony
item.description: Residents of Kalakshetra Colony in Thiruvanmiyur are facing a crisis as the GCC park on MGR Road is losing its greenery due to malfunctioning sprinklers. A committee member of the Kalakshetra Colony W...

item.title: England secure third place in World Cup with decisive victory over France, vindicating Thomas Tuchel
item.description: England's national team secured third place in the 2026 World Cup after a dramatic 6-4 victory over France in Miami. The match, which followed their heartbreaking semi-final defeat to Argentina, saw E...

item.title: NBA Election Results: Badejo-Okusanya and Afam Okeke in the Lead
item.description: The Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) election results show Senior Advocate Mrs. Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya leading with 10,554 votes out of 22,863 cast as of Saturday evening. She trails only behind La...

item.title: Wildfire Smoke and Air Quality Concerns for World Cup Final
item.description: Wildfire smoke from Canadian fires has blanketed the Northeastern United States, raising concerns about air quality for the upcoming World Cup final. Meteorologists predict that thunderstorms will hel...

item.title: Anna Friel on Lighting's Impact on Thought and Her Role in Australian Medical Drama
item.description: Anna Friel, star of Australian medical drama *The F Ward*, discusses her experiences on set, highlighting the challenges of portraying a female surgeon in a male-dominated field. She shares insights i...

item.title: Borno Gov Shows Video Of 36 Students Still Held By Terrorists, Promises More Rescue
item.description: The Borno State government has revealed a 15-second video showing 36 school students still held captive by terrorists in Lassa, Borno, nearly three weeks after their abduction. The video, played durin...

item.title: Murder investigation into South Africa's crime-fighting chief Lt-Gen Mfazi
item.description: Lt-Gen Sindile 'Pitso' Mfazi, South Africa's crime-fighting chief, died under mysterious circumstances in 2020, with his death initially attributed to Covid-19 complications. His widow, Lindelwa Mfazi...

item.title: Ben Kitili's Agricultural Ventures: Goat Farming and Orange Cultivation in Kitui
item.description: NTV news anchor Ben Kitili recently shared a rare glimpse into his life beyond the newsroom, showcasing his expansive agricultural property in Kitui known as Thua Ranch. The video, posted on TikTok un...

item.title: Inah De Belen's Mother Suggested Living with Jake Vargas Before Marriage
item.description: Inah De Belen recently shared insights about her mother, Janice De Belen's, advice regarding her relationship with Jake Vargas prior to their marriage. The revelation came during an interview where In...

item.title: Mother of Child Killed by Police in US Hospitalized in Coma
item.description: A mother in the United States has been hospitalized in a coma following the death of her child, who was killed by police officers during a confrontation. The incident has sparked widespread public out...

item.title: Public concerns about AI data centres' costs and environmental impact in Canada
item.description: A recent Leger poll reveals widespread Canadian concern over the financial and ecological consequences of expanding artificial intelligence data centres. Eighty-one percent of respondents fear these f...

item.title: ICE Officer's Troubled History Raises Vetting Concerns After Maine Shooting
item.description: A woman has claimed her abusive ex-husband, David Brouillette, is an ICE officer who shot and killed an immigrant in Maine, reigniting debates about the vetting process for immigration enforcement age...

item.title: Makati Run Raises 8,000 for Relief Funds
item.description: The Angat Buhay charity event held in Makati City successfully raised 8,000 pesos to support relief efforts for communities affected by recent disasters. Organized as part of a larger initiative to co...

item.title: Magistrates Seek Judicial Independence and Better Working Conditions
item.description: Magistrates in South Africa are pushing for greater independence from the Department of Justice (DoJ), citing concerns over administrative control, inadequate resources, and disparities in treatment c...

item.title: China Denies US Election Interference Claims, Calls Accusations 'Unfounded'
item.description: China has categorically rejected allegations by former US President Donald Trump that Beijing interfered in the 2024 US elections, describing the claims as 'unfounded' and accusing Washington of launc...

item.title: Vancouver West End Offers Dog-Friendly ATM Service
item.description: A unique service has been introduced in Vancouver’s West End, where an ATM is available for dogs to access cash without requiring a PIN. This initiative, reported by CTV News on July 18, 2026, aims to...

item.title: SRC Approves Salary Increases and New Allowance Structure for Civil Servants
item.description: The Salaries and Remuneration Commission (SRC) has approved revised salary structures and new allowances for national government civil servants, effective from 1 July 2026. The changes cover basic sal...
